name: obi_l3_zscore_fade
hypothesis: Persistent L1-L3 book imbalance predicts short-horizon mid drift toward the heavy side.
label: future_mid_return(horizon='1s')

name: microprice_dev_pull
hypothesis: Microprice deviation from mid measures latent queue pressure and pulls the next mid move.
label: future_mid_return(horizon='500ms')

name: depth_delta_bid_ask_diff
hypothesis: Asymmetric depth replenishment over 500ms windows leads price toward the strengthening side.
label: future_mid_return(horizon='2s')

name: trade_flow_imb_clip
hypothesis: Signed trade-flow imbalance over a 2s window continues into the next second of mid returns.
label: future_mid_return(horizon='1s')

name: tight_spread_obi_regime
hypothesis: Book imbalance is only informative when the spread is tight; wide spreads mask the signal.
label: future_mid_return(horizon='1s')

name: bad_family_momentum
hypothesis: Family value is not in the frozen six-family enum so schema validation must reject it.
label: future_mid_return(horizon='1s')

name: bad_parse_unbalanced
hypothesis: Signal formula has an unbalanced parenthesis and must die with a formula parse error.
label: future_mid_return(horizon='1s')

name: bad_unknown_primitive_vwap
hypothesis: Uses a vwap primitive that is not part of prim_v1 so it must die as primitive invalid.
label: future_mid_return(horizon='1s')

name: bad_uses_proposed_queue_age
hypothesis: Proposes a queue_age primitive and then actually calls it, which v1 must refuse to execute.
label: future_mid_return(horizon='1s')

proposed_new_primitives.name: queue_age
proposed_new_primitives.reason: queue position aging is not expressible with prim_v1 primitives

name: bad_levels_out_of_range
hypothesis: Requests nine book levels but the panel only carries five, so the argument is invalid.
label: future_mid_return(horizon='1s')

name: bad_nested_too_deep
hypothesis: Stacks four transform layers over a primitive, exceeding the call depth limit of three.
label: future_mid_return(horizon='1s')

name: dup_renamed_obi_l3
hypothesis: Identical formula to the first candidate with renamed feature, so the formula hash collides.
label: future_mid_return(horizon='1s')
